rationale: Ground truth is a trivial clean rename PR (lgtm, no comments). X correctly approves with well-grounded verification (checked upstream matches, confirmed no missed occurrences, noted test coverage) and no fabricated issues. Y invents two low-value nits: one asks to justify a naming divergence that the PR title itself already explains as the entire point of the fix, and the other flags `openai_serving_audio_generate` as inconsistently unrenamed, conflating an unrelated attribute with the tokenization-specific upstream alignment this PR targets — a misunderstanding of PR scope that hurts precision.
